decisions.title: Commit to Explore V2 GA date and deprecation timeline for V1
decisions.context: ENG-8140 migration work is in progress. Need to set hard cutover date to force completion and align customer comms. V2 is core to 'Nail the Core' pillar.

arguments.for: Clear deadline drives focus, enables customer migration planning, unlocks resource reallocation from V1 maintenance
arguments.against: May need to extend if critical bugs emerge, customer adoption metrics still unclear

decisions.title: Prioritize memory leak fix (ENG-9344) as P0 or accept degraded export experience
decisions.context: Exports leaking memory directly impacts core product reliability. Affects customer trust and 'Nail the Core'. Need resource commitment or scope cut decision.

arguments.for: Exports are table stakes feature, memory issues cause cascading failures, customer escalations likely
arguments.against: Resources stretched on V2 migration, may be edge case usage pattern, workaround via smaller exports exists

decisions.title: Ship Family Stats feature or cut scope to unblock Q1 milestone
decisions.context: Multiple Family Stats tickets (ENG-9394, ENG-9419, ENG-9379, ENG-9067) indicate feature nearing completion but still buggy. Need ship/cut decision to maintain velocity.

arguments.for: Feature aligns with 'Decisions Captured' north star by surfacing actionable insights, customer demand validated
arguments.against: Quality concerns across multiple components, may erode trust if shipped buggy, could defer to Q2

decisions.title: Define K2 Mission scope and resource allocation or deprioritize
decisions.context: ENG-9104 pipeline work started, ENG-9165 EKS deployment in progress, but unclear strategic fit. Is this 'Build the Moat' or distraction from core?
decisions.blockedBy: Unclear alignment with 2026 strategy pillars, no defined success metrics, competing with Explore V2 resources

arguments.for: Could be infrastructure for 'Embed Deeper' via new data sources, technical exploration valuable
arguments.against: Not on 2026 roadmap, diverts engineering from Nail the Core focus, no customer signal

decisions.title: Standardize double value handling across product (ENG-9347) or document limitation
decisions.context: MAX_SAFE_INT issues suggest systemic data type problem. Fix requires cross-product work. Decision: comprehensive fix vs targeted warnings vs accept limitation.

arguments.for: Prevents data integrity issues at scale, builds customer trust, aligns with quality-first approach
arguments.against: Large refactor risk, may not affect majority of customers, warnings may suffice as interim solution

timeAudit.weekSummary: 85% meeting load—significantly above healthy threshold. Only 6 focus hours for strategic work. Wednesday has zero focus blocks.
alerts: Thursday 14:00-17:00: Three back-to-back meetings with no break

1:1 with Eng Lead rescheduled twice this week—needs stable slot
